title: Ficus calyptroceras
taxonomy: Ficus calyptroceras is classified in the plant kingdom, specifically within the phylum Magnoliophyta, and further classified as a member of the class Magnoliopsida. It belongs to the family Moraceae, which includes various fig species. The genus Ficus is a group of tropical plants known for their figs, which are an important food source for many animals. Ficus calyptroceras is a species of fig tree native to tropical regions. Its classification also includes being a part of the order Rosales, which comprises various flowering plant families.

intro: Ficus calyptroceras, also known as the fig tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Moraceae, native to tropical Asia and widely cultivated for its medicinal and edible properties. The leaves and fruits of Ficus calyptroceras are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Ficus calyptroceras include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ficus calyptroceras is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in folk remedies for its purported health benefits.
